originalTitle: Angry crowd sets Ebola hospital tents on fire in DR Congo
title: A Sad Day in Congo: Learning About Sickness and Helping Each Other
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa and beyond! It's your friendly kid reporter here, bringing you some important news from a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. Sometimes, big and sad things happen, and it's good to learn about them in a simple way so we can all understand and help make the world a better place.

A Sad Goodbye

Imagine someone you love very much gets very sick. And then, sadly, they pass away. When this happens, families often want to say goodbye in a special way, a way that has been part of their culture for many, many years. It's a very important time for them. This is what happened in a place in DR Congo. A person got sick, and sadly, they died. The person might have had a very tricky sickness called Ebola.

What is Ebola?

Ebola is a sickness that can make people very, very ill. It's not like a common cold. It's much more serious. The amazing doctors and nurses work super hard to help people who get Ebola. **Did you know?** Ebola is a sickness that doctors found a long time ago near a river called the Ebola River. That's how it got its name! Ebola is very special because it can spread super fast, even after someone has passed away. If you touch someone who had Ebola, or even their clothes, you can get sick too. That's why doctors have to be extra, extra careful.

Why Doctors Need to Be Extra Careful

When someone passes away from Ebola, the germs are still there. If family and friends touch the body during a traditional goodbye ceremony, many more people could get sick. And we don't want that! So, the doctors and health workers have a very important job. They have to make sure that the goodbye ceremony is done in a very special way, a safe way, so that the sickness doesn't spread to anyone else. It's not because they want to be mean; it's because they want to protect everyone. **Did you know?** Washing your hands with soap and water for 20 seconds can help wash away many germs and keep you healthy! That's like singing "Happy Birthday" twice!

A Very Upset Crowd

In DR Congo, the sad family and friends of the person who passed away wanted to take their loved one home. They wanted to have their special goodbye party, their traditional burial, just like they always do. But the doctors and health workers had to say no. They explained that because it might have been Ebola, they needed to do a special, safe burial to keep everyone else safe. This made the family and friends very, very sad. And then, because they were so sad and upset, they got very angry. When people are very upset and angry, they sometimes do things they might not normally do.

When Anger Takes Over

Because they were so angry and sad, some people in the crowd did something that caused more problems. They went to where the doctors had put up some special tents, like temporary doctor's offices, to help sick people. These tents were there to help people get better and to stop the sickness from spreading. But in their anger, some people lit fires and burned these tents. This was a very sad thing to happen. It meant that the doctors and nurses, who are like superheroes trying to save lives, had to stop helping people in those tents. It also made it harder to care for others who might get sick. **Did you know?** Doctors and nurses often work very long hours, even away from their own families, to take care of people who are sick. They are true heroes!

Why It's Hard to Say Goodbye Safely

We all understand that saying goodbye to someone you love is very important. Every family and every culture has its own special ways to do this. These traditions bring comfort and help people remember their loved ones. It must have been incredibly hard for the family to be told they couldn't follow their traditions. But the doctors were thinking about everyone in the village, trying to keep them all healthy and safe from a very scary sickness. It's a very tricky situation where people's sad feelings and important traditions clash with the need to stop a dangerous sickness.

What Happens Now?

After the tents were burned, it made the job of the doctors and health workers even harder. But these brave people don't give up. They want to help everyone stay healthy. They are still working very hard to teach people about Ebola and how to stay safe. They want to make sure that fewer people get sick and that everyone knows what to do if they or someone they know feels unwell. **Did you know?** There are many different countries in Africa, and each one has its own beautiful cultures and traditions! The Democratic Republic of Congo is a very big country in Central Africa.

Learning and Helping Each Other

This story reminds us that sometimes, when we are sad or scared, it's hard to listen and understand. But it's super important to try our best to listen to the people who are trying to help us, especially doctors and health workers. They are the ones who know the most about how to fight sickness and keep our communities safe. We can all help by being kind to each other, especially when people are sad. We can also learn about staying healthy, like washing our hands often. And if we ever hear about a sickness like Ebola, we should listen to what the health experts say. Our communities are strong when we work together, even when things are difficult. Let's remember to be patient, kind, and always try to understand each other. Thank you for listening, my friends. Until next time, stay safe and keep learning!
summary: A very sad family in DR Congo got very angry and burned doctor tents because they couldn't have a special goodbye for their loved one who might have had Ebola, a sickness that needs very careful goodbyes to keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how sadness and old traditions can make it hard for doctors to stop sickness from spreading, and why we need to listen to health helpers to keep everyone safe and strong.

originalTitle: Dozens of vehicles burnt as Mali jihadists enforce blockade ahead of Eid holiday
title: A Big Worry in Mali: Blocked Roads and Missing Food!
content:
Hello, My Young Friends!

Hello, amazing friends! It's me, your kid-friendly news reporter for Africa! Today, we are going on an imaginary journey to a beautiful country called Mali. Mali is in West Africa, on our wonderful continent. It's a place with kind people and lots of sunshine.

What is Happening in Mali?

Something a little bit sad is happening there right now. Some roads are closed. This is called a "blockade." Imagine someone closing the big gate to your school or your playground. It means cars and trucks cannot go in or out easily. Some cars and trucks have even been stopped. This has caused big problems for many families in Mali.

What is a "Blockade"?

A blockade is like a big, invisible wall on the roads. It stops things from moving. Think about your toys. If you put a wall in front of your toy cars, they cannot move, right? That is what a blockade does on real roads. It means people cannot get to places they need to go. And very important things like food and fuel cannot travel on the roads.

Why is this Happening?

There are some groups of people who are causing trouble in Mali. They are making it hard for other people. They want to stop things from moving freely. These groups are making many people sad and worried.

Not Enough "Car Food"!

Because of the blockade, there is not enough "car food" in Mali. What is "car food"? It's called fuel! Fuel is what cars, buses, and trucks need to move. Just like you need food to run and play, cars need fuel to go fast and far. When there is no fuel, cars cannot move. Buses cannot take children to school. Trucks cannot bring fresh food from farms to the shops. This makes life very difficult for everyone. Did you know? Some cars can run on sunshine! They are called solar cars. But most cars use fuel like petrol or diesel.

Not Enough Yummy Food!

The blockade also means there is not enough yummy food in some places. Trucks usually bring tasty fruits, vegetables, and other good food from farms to cities. But if trucks cannot move, then food cannot get to the shops. Imagine going to the market, and there are no bananas or no bread! That would be very sad, right? People in Mali are worried about having enough food for their families. They need food to eat and be strong every day.

A Special Celebration is Coming!

Soon, there is a very special holiday for many people in Mali. It is called Eid! Eid is a time for happiness and family. It is like a big party. Families cook delicious food, wear new clothes, and visit their friends. Children get to play special games and sometimes even get small gifts! It is a time for sharing and feeling joyful. Eid is a very important holiday for Muslim people around the world. Did you know? Eid is celebrated by millions of people across Africa and the whole world! They all share in the happiness of this special time.

Why is the Blockade Extra Sad Now?

This blockade makes Eid even harder for families. They need fuel to visit their relatives in other towns. They need food to cook yummy meals and share with guests. When there are shortages, it is hard to celebrate this happy time. Families want to share and have fun, but they are worried. It is like planning a birthday party, but then you cannot get the cake or the balloons. They wish for everything to be normal for Eid.

What Happened to the Cars and Trucks?

Sadly, some of the trucks and cars that were trying to bring fuel and food were stopped. Some of them were even damaged badly. This made it even harder for things to move around. It is a very sad thing to happen to useful vehicles. These vehicles are important for helping people.

Brave People and Helping Hands

Even when things are hard, people in Mali are brave. They are trying to help each other. Friends are sharing what they have, like extra food or water. Neighbors are looking out for each other and offering help. This is called "community spirit." When we face problems, it is important to be kind and help our friends. This makes everyone feel a little bit better. People are hoping that the roads will open again soon and safely. Did you know? Mali is famous for its beautiful music and ancient cities like Timbuktu, which has very old and wise libraries!

What Can We Wish For?

We can all send our kind thoughts to the children and families in Mali. We wish for the roads to open safely. We wish for fuel and food to reach everyone who needs it. And we wish for a happy Eid for all the families there. Let's hope for peace and smiles in Mali soon! We send them our love from all over Africa.
summary: Roads in Mali are blocked by trouble-makers, making it hard for people to get fuel and food, especially before the happy Eid holiday.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone needs food and fuel to live happily and move around, and it's sad when people can't get what they need, especially during a special holiday for families.

originalTitle: DR Congo cancels World Cup training camp over Ebola outbreak
title: DR Congo cancels World Cup training camp over Ebola outbreak
content: The outbreak, caused by a rare Ebola species, is thought to have caused 139 deaths so far.

originalTitle: Ronaldo scores twice to seal Saudi Pro League at last with Al-Nassr
title: Superstar Ronaldo Helps Team Win Big Championship!
content:
Welcome, Super Readers!

Hello, hello, happy friends! Your friendly African news reporter is here! Guess what exciting news we have today? It's all about football! Yes, that super fun game where players kick a round ball into a net. We have some amazing news from a place called Saudi Arabia. It's about a very, very famous football player!

Who is This Amazing Player?

His name is Cristiano Ronaldo! Have you heard of him? He is like a superhero on the football field! He is from a country far away called Portugal. Ronaldo is known all over the world because he is super good at scoring goals. He can kick the ball so fast and so accurately! Many children in Africa and all over the world look up to Ronaldo. They dream of playing football just like him when they grow up. He works very, very hard to be the best player he can be!

What is Football?

Let's talk a little about football, or soccer, as some people call it. It's a game played by two teams. Each team has eleven players on the field at one time. The main goal is to kick the ball into the other team's net. When you kick the ball into the net, it's called a "goal," and your team gets a point! The team with the most goals at the end of the game wins. It's a game that needs speed, strength, and a lot of teamwork. Everyone on the team needs to work together, like a big, happy family! **Did you know?** Football is the most popular sport in the whole wide world! Billions of people love to watch and play it everywhere, from big stadiums to small villages!

Ronaldo's Team: Al-Nassr!

Cristiano Ronaldo plays for a team called Al-Nassr. This team is from a country named Saudi Arabia. It's a very big country far away, in a part of the world called the Middle East. Al-Nassr plays in a big competition there, like a super-fun championship for all the best teams! Teams play many, many games throughout the year in this competition. It's called the "Saudi Pro League." Winning the league means you are the best football team in that country for the whole year! Imagine being the best at something for a whole year! That's a super big achievement.

The Big, Exciting Game!

So, Ronaldo and his team, Al-Nassr, were playing a very important game. It was a game that could help them win the whole league championship! Imagine the excitement building up! Everyone was watching, cheering very loudly for their favorite team. The players were running fast, kicking the ball, and trying their very best to score. The crowd was roaring with every kick and pass! Ronaldo, being the superstar he is, did something truly amazing! He scored not just one goal, but two fantastic goals in that game! Yes, two fantastic goals that made all his teammates and fans jump with joy. Scoring goals is very hard, but Ronaldo makes it look easy because he practices a lot, every single day.

Al-Nassr Wins the Championship!

Because of Ronaldo's amazing goals and the fantastic hard work of all his teammates, Al-Nassr won the game! And guess what? Winning that game meant they won the entire "Saudi Pro League" championship! Hooray for Al-Nassr! It was a very, very special moment for Al-Nassr and all their supporters, who had been cheering all season. They had worked hard all season long, played many games, and finally, they reached their big dream. They lifted the big, shiny trophy, which is like a giant cup, to show everyone they were the champions! Everyone cheered so loudly! **Did you know?** A "league title" means a team has finished at the top of their competition after playing many games against different teams. It's a very big prize for being the most consistent and best team throughout the whole season!

Why is This News Special?

This news is special because it shows us many important things. First, it shows how talented Cristiano Ronaldo is. He is truly one of the best football players ever to kick a ball! Second, it shows the power of teamwork. Even with a superstar like Ronaldo, everyone on the team has to play together, pass the ball, and help each other to win. It also shows that if you work very hard and never give up, you can reach your goals and dreams. Ronaldo trains every day. He eats healthy food and rests well. That's why he can play so well and score so many goals. It teaches us that practice makes perfect, and trying your best always pays off! **Did you know?** Ronaldo has won many, many awards in his career. He has even won the "Ballon d'Or" multiple times, which is a very special award for the best football player in the entire world!

Dreams and Hard Work

For all you young football fans out there, this story is a wonderful reminder. If you love something, like playing football with your friends, keep practicing! Keep running, keep kicking, keep learning with your teammates. Who knows, maybe one day you could be a famous football player too, just like Ronaldo! Ronaldo's win with Al-Nassr is a happy story for all who love the beautiful game of football. It's a reminder that sports bring people together from all over the world, and that working hard always, always pays off. Keep cheering for your favorite teams, and maybe one day, we will cheer for you! What an exciting story about Ronaldo and Al-Nassr! We hope you enjoyed this happy news. Keep smiling and keep playing!
summary: Cristiano Ronaldo, a famous football superstar, scored two amazing goals to help his team, Al-Nassr, win the Saudi Pro League championship, making many fans around the world very happy and excited.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how hard work and teamwork can help you achieve great things, like winning a big championship. It also inspires many young football fans to chase their dreams and practice hard, just like Ronaldo.

originalTitle: Ebola treatment hospital burned down in DRC as outbreak spreads
title: Ebola treatment hospital burned down in DRC as outbreak spreads
content: Protesters set a hospital on fire in the Democratic of Congo, as authorities struggle to contain an Ebola outbreak.

originalTitle: NYC launches lottery for $50 World Cup football tickets
title: Yay! Cheaper World Cup Tickets for Many!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa here! Today, we have some super exciting news about the biggest football party in the world! Are you ready? Let's go!

What is Football?

First, let's talk about football! Do you know what football is? It's a super fun game where two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's goal. Everyone loves football! It makes people jump and cheer! **Did you know?** Football is also called soccer in some parts of the world, like America! But it's the same wonderful game!

The Amazing World Cup!

Now, imagine the *biggest* football party ever! That's the World Cup! It's when teams from all over the world come together to play. Countries send their best players to compete. It's like a giant celebration of sport and friendship! The World Cup happens only every four years. That means it's very special! Everyone gets excited when it's time for the World Cup.

Where is New York City?

Our news today comes from a far-away city called New York City. It's in a country called America. New York City is a very, very big city with tall buildings and lots of people. It's like a busy beehive! **Did you know?** New York City is sometimes called "The Big Apple"! No, it's not a real apple, it's just a fun nickname!

World Cup Coming to New York!

Guess what? The World Cup is coming to New York City! Yes, some of the big games will be played there. Imagine how exciting it will be! People will come from everywhere to watch their favourite teams play.

What are Tickets?

To go and watch a big football game, you usually need a "ticket." A ticket is like a special paper that lets you get inside the stadium. Stadiums are huge places where games are played. Tickets can sometimes be very expensive, especially for super important games like the World Cup! **Did you know?** The biggest football stadium in the world can hold over 100,000 people! That's like a whole town watching a game!

Special Tickets for Less Money!

Here's the super cool part of our news! New York City is making some tickets much, much cheaper. They are usually very expensive, but these special tickets will only cost $50. That's a great price! It means more people might get to go and watch the games.

What is a Lottery?

But how do they decide who gets these special cheaper tickets? They are using something called a "lottery." A lottery is a fair way to choose. Imagine putting everyone's name into a big hat. Then, someone closes their eyes and picks names out. The people whose names are picked get the special tickets! New York City is going to pick 1,000 lucky people to get these tickets. One thousand! That's a lot of happy football fans!

Why is This Good News?

This is good news because it means more people can enjoy the magic of the World Cup. Football is for everyone! When tickets are cheaper, it helps more families and kids go see the games. Everyone deserves a chance to cheer for their team! **Did you know?** African teams play in the World Cup too! We cheer very loudly for our amazing teams from countries like Senegal, Morocco, and Ghana! They make us very proud!

The Joy of World Cup!

The World Cup is not just about football. It's about bringing people together. It's about cheering, laughing, and making new friends. It's about seeing amazing skills and unforgettable goals! Even if you can't go to New York City, you can still watch the games on TV or listen on the radio. It's an exciting time for the whole world! **Did you know?** The World Cup trophy is made of real gold! It's very shiny and special, and only the winning team gets to hold it high!

Our Football Dreams

Maybe one day, you or your friends will play in the World Cup! Keep practicing your football skills. Keep running, kicking, and scoring! Who knows? Your dream could come true! This news from New York City reminds us that football brings joy to everyone, no matter where they are. It's a game that connects us all.

Final Thoughts from Your Reporter

So, there you have it, my little champions! Exciting news about cheaper World Cup tickets in faraway New York City, chosen by a fair lottery! Isn't that wonderful? Keep loving football, and keep dreaming big! This is your African kid-friendly news reporter, signing off! See you next time!
summary: New York City is giving 1,000 lucky people a chance to buy special, cheaper World Cup football tickets for only $50, picked by a lottery!
whyMatters: This is important because it means more people can go to the big World Cup games and enjoy the fun, even if they don't have lots of money. It makes the World Cup fair for everyone!

originalTitle: Angry crowd sets Ebola hospital tents on fire in DR Congo
title: Working Together to Keep Everyone Safe!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! Your friendly news reporter, Pendo, is here from sunny Africa! I hope you are all doing super well and ready to learn something important today. We are going to talk about a special place called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. It's a big, beautiful country in Africa, just like many of our homes!

What is Ebola?

Sometimes, people can get very, very sick. One sickness we talk about is called Ebola. It's a kind of germ that can make people feel very unwell. Imagine when you have a cold or a cough, but much, much stronger. Ebola germs can spread from one person to another if they touch someone who is very sick. That's why doctors and nurses work extra hard to keep everyone safe when someone has Ebola. They are like superheroes protecting us! Did you know? Germs are tiny, tiny things that you cannot see with your eyes, but they can make you sick! That's why washing your hands often with soap and water is so important!

Special Doctors and Hospitals

When someone gets sick with Ebola, they need lots of help. There are special doctors and nurses who are trained to take care of them. These brave helpers work in special hospitals or clinics. They wear special suits that cover their whole bodies. These suits are like a shield to stop the germs from getting on them. This way, they can help the sick person without getting sick themselves. Did you know? Doctors and nurses are like real-life heroes! They work hard every day to make sure people get better. They learn a lot to help us!

Why Are There Rules?

Now, these special doctors and nurses have very important rules. These rules are not to be mean, but to keep *everyone* safe. Imagine if you are playing a game; you need rules so everyone plays fairly and nobody gets hurt, right? It's the same with health! One of the most important rules is about how to care for someone who has Ebola, even after they have passed away. This is a very sad time for families and friends. Did you know? Rules help us live together happily and safely in our communities, schools, and even at home!

When Someone Passes Away

It is very, very sad when someone we love gets sick and passes away. Families feel a lot of pain and miss their loved ones so much. When a person passes away from Ebola, their body still has some of the sickness germs. These germs can still spread to other people if they touch the body. So, to keep everyone safe, doctors have very special ways to take care of the body. They make sure the germs don't spread to family and friends during burial. This means families cannot always do their usual burial customs right away. This can make people even sadder and sometimes confused or upset, because they want to say goodbye in their traditional way. Did you know? Many cultures around Africa and the world have special ways to remember people who have passed away. These ways help families say goodbye and feel better.

What Happened in DR Congo

In a place in DR Congo, there was a very sad time. A person passed away from Ebola. Their family and friends were heartbroken. They loved this person very much. They wanted to take their loved one home to bury them in their own way. But the doctors knew that because of the Ebola germs, this would not be safe for the family or the community. They had to follow the special rules to stop the sickness from spreading. Sometimes, when people are very, very sad, and maybe don't fully understand *why* these special rules are so important, they can get very, very upset. In their sadness and confusion, some people became angry. This made things very difficult. Some parts of the special hospital, where doctors were working hard to help people, got damaged because people were so upset. This made it even harder for the kind doctors and nurses to help other sick people. It’s important to remember that everyone was sad, and sometimes sadness can make people do things they might not normally do. But it also shows us how important it is to listen and understand each other.

Why We Listen to Doctors

It’s super important to listen to doctors and nurses. They are the experts! They know how to fight the germs and keep us all safe and healthy. When they tell us about rules for sickness like Ebola, they are trying to protect everyone in the community. They want to stop the germs from spreading so that no more people get sick. By listening to them, we are all helping to keep our families, friends, and neighbours safe. Did you know? When you listen to your parents, teachers, and doctors, you are being a responsible and smart helper in your community!

Being a Helper

We can all be helpers! Even you, amazing kids! How? You can wash your hands often with soap and water, especially before eating and after playing. You can tell an adult if you don't feel well. You can be kind and understanding to people who are sad or sick. And you can listen to your adults and doctors when they share important health information. When we all work together, we can stop germs like Ebola from spreading. We can keep our communities strong and healthy. It means being patient and trusting the people who are there to help. Did you know? Helping others makes you feel happy too! It's like a superpower!

Working Together for Health

So, remember, my friends, it’s all about working together. Doctors, families, friends, and even you! When we face big challenges like sickness, listening to each other and understanding the rules helps everyone stay safe. We all want to live in a healthy and happy Africa. By being kind, understanding, and listening to our doctors, we can make sure that happens! Let's all be health heroes together!
summary: In DR Congo, doctors have special rules for Ebola to keep everyone safe, but sometimes people get very sad and upset when they don't understand why these rules are important, leading to problems at hospitals.
whyMatters: It's important to listen to doctors and work together to stop sickness like Ebola from spreading, so everyone in our community can stay healthy and happy.

title: Arteta told of title win by crying son - while having barbecue
